rulings.Case Title: JUNE AURE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Deft. Baggage Systems, Inc.'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 2, 2015, Line 10, Defendant Baggage Systems, Inc.'s Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer. The motion to amend answer is denied. Defendant was dilatory in conducting discovery and bringing this motion. See Duchrow v. Forrest (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 1359, 1377. The verified answer pleads statute of limitations defenses and such defenses can be raised in motions in limine, and requests for applicable jury instructions based on evidence produced at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE GODDARD VS. JANICE FURMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 2, 2015, Line 4, Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The motion to amend is denied. Mr. Abdullah does not rely on a pleading defect in his motion for summary judgment and appears to indicate that the complaint is well-pleaded as to him. See CCP 452. Accordingly, the amendment is unnecessary. In addition, allowing amendment at this juncture will prejudice Mr. Abdullah. An amended complaint will render his pending motion for summary judgment moot. See State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Sup. Crt. 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. Mr. Abdullah will be required to file a new motion for summary judgment with at least 75-days notice, pay a new filing fee, and it is unclear whether the court would allow its calendaring based on the current trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: WILDA L WHITE VS. GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 2, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF WILDA WHITE's Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories; And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Wilda White seeks a further response to Form Interrogatory 17.1 which seeks facts and documents supporting Defendant's denial of a request for admission alleging a breach of the STANDARD OF CARE by Defendant in the treatment of Plaintiff. In a medical malpractice case, the issue of the STANDARD OF CARE is a matter of expert opinion. Under County of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (1990) 224 Cal. App.3d 1446,1455-56, while questions to a defendant physician about their impressions and reasons for action or lack of action at the time the medical procedure was performed are¿appropriate", a physician who is the subject of the malpractice action is not required to provide opinion testimony unless the physician is designated as an expert witness ("The present expert opinions of a party physician concerning the care given are irrelevant unless the physician is designated as an expert witness.") Because the underlying Request for Admission necessarily implicates expert opinion testimony and not facts or details of treatment, the motion is DENIED. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sanfordkingsley@yahoo.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRYAN LOCKWOOD VS. TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Futher Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 2, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED in part as follows: Request for Production #3 (driver's licenses, voter registration cards, passports) is overbroad as to scope and time and is denied. Request for Production #4 (documents evidencing places of domicile and residence for Plaintiff and Plaintiff's wife for 2004 to present) is relevant but overbroad. The documents to be produced are limited to those which identify any place of residence for Plaintiff during the four years prior to October 9, 2013. The documents to be produced are further limited to those which set forth or document Plaintiff's right to occupy the place of residence such as a letter of permission, grant deed, lease or rental agreement. Request for Production #5 (documents evidencing real estate owned by Plaintiff and or Plaintiff's wife) is overbroad since it has no requirement of residence by Plaintiff and is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sanfordkingsley@yahoo.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ADELIA G MEDEL VS. STEPHEN HO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion In Opposition To Defendant Stephen Hos Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 2, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion In Opposition To Defendant Stephen Ho's Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And Proof Of Service. The motion is granted. The record does not disclose substantial competent evidence showing that the settlement is in the ballpark within the meaning of Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n. 6. While Mr. Ho is paying his insurance policy limit, there is no evidence regarding his financial status. There is also insufficient evidence regarding a rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery. CCSF is correct that plaintiff claims over $200,000 in medical bills. It also appears that Mr. Ho is mostly at fault with respect to the accident. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PETER MCDONALD ET AL VS. 830 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 2, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT 830 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court denies the anti-SLAPP motion to strike. Defendant's statements to Plaintiff Victoria's email did not trigger the first prong of CCP 425.16. Section 425.16, subdivision (e) defines an " 'act in furtherance of a person's right of petition or free speech under the United States or California Constitution in connection with a public issue' " to include, among others: "(3) any written or oral statement or writing made in a place open to the public or a public forum in connection with an issue of public interest . . ." There is no public forum involved because Plaintiff's email is a private email communication of limited membership to request contact information for two Board members. See Perlow v. Mann (C.D. Cal. Oct. 22, 2013) WL5727259 at *4. There is nothing in the email to indicate that it was intended to serve as a place where members could communicate their ideas. Even if the Court assumes that the emails are a public forum as defined in CCP § 425.16, D still fails to meet the first prong of CCP § 425.16 because D fails to show that the emails concern a matter of public interest. Spanle's comments are not a matter of public interest because they are verbal attacks on P's character. The Court does not entertain the statute of limitations argument raised for the first time in the Reply. First, it should have been raised in the moving papers. Second, the statute of limitations issue goes to the second prong of the anti-SLAPP statute. Defendants fail to meet the first prong of the statute, however. Plaintiff is entitled to attorney's fees pursuant to motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
